name: chembl
description: ChEMBL — bioactive molecules, drug targets, bioactivity data, approved drugs, and chemical structures. Backed by the public ChEMBL REST API (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chembl/api/data/). Vendored from the google-deepmind/science-skills bundle.
author: zorai

status.description: Check ChEMBL API status and version.

molecule.description: Query the molecule endpoint. Search by SMILES / ChEMBL ID / InChIKey, or fetch a single molecule by --id.

target.description: Query the target endpoint (proteins, nucleic acids, complexes annotated with bioactivity data).

activity.description: Query the activity endpoint. Returns IC50 / Ki / etc. for (compound, target) pairs. Use --normalize to convert to nM.

assay.description: Query the assay endpoint (experimental protocols that produced bioactivity measurements).

drug.description: Query the drug endpoint (approved drugs with mechanism, indication, and trade names).

mechanism.description: Query the mechanism endpoint (drug → target → action type → mechanism-of-action).

similarity.description: Server-side similarity search. Returns molecules with Tanimoto >= the threshold (default 0.7) to the query SMILES.

substructure.description: Server-side substructure search. Returns molecules containing the query SMILES as a substructure.

image.description: Download a compound image (SVG by default, or PNG) for a given ChEMBL molecule ID.

python.command: uv run scripts/chembl_api.py image ${CH_ID:?set CH_ID (ChEMBL molecule ID like CHEMBL25)} ${CH_FORMAT:+--format $CH_FORMAT} ${CH_OUTPUT:+--output $CH_OUTPUT}

run.description: Generic catch-all: forward an arbitrary chembl_api.py subcommand. Use this for endpoints not exposed as named commands (assay_class, atc_class, binding_site, biotherapeutic, cell_line, chembl_id_lookup, chembl_release, compound_record, compound_structural_alert, document, document_similarity, drug_indication, drug_warning, go_slim, metabolism, molecule_form, organism, protein_classification, source, target_component, target_relation, tissue, xref_source). Required env: CH_SUBCOMMAND. Other args pass through to the script's flag parser.
